info.description: Web service that allows you to obtain a crif search on a person or a company and personal information about a natural or legal person such as personal data, telephones and the presence of negative events.

method.description: Indicate through which method you want to receive data with the callback, by default it is POST

field.description: Name of the field containing the callback return data (required with method: POST)

url.description: Url where the callback call will be made

headers.description: contains key:value strings that you want to arrive as headers

data.description: Object to be completed at your convenience with additional data that you can use back with the callback

text_pdf.description: Custom description of the first page of the negativity report in PDF format

title_pdf.description: Custom title of the negativity report in PDF format

logo_pdf.description: Custom logo for the negative report in PDF format (must be passed via URL)

NewCallback.description: This is our standardized OpenAPI callback system. It enables the configuration of parameters for receiving notifications about status updates of asynchronous requests to this endpoint.

method.description: Specify the callback method. When set to 'POST', a standard HTTP POST request will be initiated with the Content-Type header set to application/x-www-form-urlencoded. The JSON data will be encoded into a key-value pair named data (customizable using the field parameter) within the request body. If the method is set to 'JSON', a direct HTTP POST request will be made with the Content-Type header set to application/json. The raw JSON data will be directly inserted into the request body without any encoding.

field.description: This parameter works in conjunction with the 'method' parameter set to 'POST'. When provided as a string, it determines the name of the parameter that will be sent in the POST request.

url.description: A valid URL capable of handling POST requests must be entered into this parameter. For endpoints requiring authentication, the 'headers' parameter can be used to specify 'basic auth' or 'bearer auth'. The maximum timeout is 30 seconds. If 'retry' is set, the system will retry up to 'retry' times until a 200 status code is received.

retry.description: The maximum number of attempts is defined by the 'retry' parameter. If the maximum number of attempts is reached without a successful status code (e.g., 200 OK), the request is considered a final failure. A time interval is often introduced between attempts to avoid overloading the server. This interval may increase exponentially with each attempt to reduce the impact on an overloaded server. The 'retry' parameter is configured to retry the request only in case of specific status codes, typically those indicating temporary errors (e.g., 502, 503) or network issues.

headers.description: A collection of HTTP headers to be sent with the request. Each header is defined as a key-value pair. Headers can be used for various purposes, including authentication, content negotiation, and custom metadata.

custom.description: This object can be populated with any additional data you wish to include in the callback response. This data will be accessible within the 'custom' parameter of the callback function. You can use this to store and retrieve any relevant information that you need for your application.

autorenew.description: to set the auto-renewal (after 1 year), by default it is true

removeDeceased.description: if you want to remove deceased entities

autorenew.description: to set the auto-renewal (after 1 year), by default it is true

list_description.description: a description of the list in which this evidence has been found

date_start.description: indicates the start date assigned by the source (formatted as [[DD.]MM.]YYYY)

offence.description: this field is used to define the offence in which the entity is involved

inquiry.description: this field is used to define the inquiry in which the entity is involved

subject.description: this field categorizes the reason why this entity is connected to the evidence

country.description: the country of the location (ISO3 format)

locations.description: this list contains all Locations associated with the entity, for example it may contain a reference or the place of birth

details.description: this field is used to describe in more detail the involvement of the entity

date_end.description: the date in which the entity is no longer being mentioned in the source or the date that the source sets as date_end (formatted as [[DD.]MM.]YYYY)

links.description: this list contains the URLs of the webpage associated with the rationales

facts.description: this field is used to define the facts in which the entity is involved

status.description: this field is used to define the status of the rationale in which the entity is involved
status.example: (non-exhaustive list of Conviction, Acquittal, Allegations/Investigations, etc.)

publication_date.description: the dates in which the information has been published by the source (formatted as [[DD.]MM.]YYYY)

source.description: this field defines where the information has been found; it might be a newspaper, an authority, or a website

list.description: the list name given from the source in which this evidence has been found (e.g., Top Ten Most Wanted - FBI)

organization.description: The organization is a Legal Entity (type L). The organization field of an evidence will be valorized if the organization's information has been collected. Organization (in the PEP dataset); the entity in which the politically exposed person performs his or her public function. NB. the organization and the source may be different parties; the organization is the party that issued the information while the source is the party that published the news.

codes.description: this list contains all the codes associated with the entity; each object contains the actual code and the type of code associated (e.g., Passport, ID Document, LEI Code)

death_dates.description: this field contains the death dates associated with the entity, each object contains the day, month and year separated in three different fields as it's also possible to have a death_date object that only contains the year or only the year and the month (however the year will always be set). This object also contains an additional field inferred, this field is used when the death date is uncertain

gender.description: this field contains the gender of the entity (Male, Female, Unknown/not a person)

organization_details.description: this field contains the detail of the organization. Only if entity_type = 'L' the field can be valued; if valued, it will contain the details of the organization whose main properties have been expressed in the other fields

organization_subtype.description: defines the organization sub-type of the organization (e.g., central+direct, central+indirect, regional+direct, etc.)

organization_type.description: defines the organization type of the organization (e.g., State Owned Enterprise, Independent Authority, etc.)

sector.description: defines the sector of the organization using the Global Industry Classification Standard

population.description: represents the population of the organization (i.e., number of people resident in the municipality)

birth_dates.description: this field contains the birthdates associated with the entity; each object contains the day, month and year separated in three different fields as it's also possible to have a birth_date object that only contains the year or only the year and the month (however the year will always be set, birth dates never contain only the day or day and month with no year indicated). This object also contains an additional field "inferred", this field is used when the birthdate is uncertain (for example in cases where the source does only specify an age)

nationalities.description: this list contains the nationalities (as String) associated with the entity

full_name.description: the full name of the entity

native.description: if set to true this field defines that this name is written in non-Latin characters

last_name.description: the last name of the entity

title.description: the title of the entity (Mr, Ms, etc.)

first_name.description: the first name of the entity

primary.description: if set to true this field defines that this name record is the primary of this entity. This is merely a suggestion for the visualization of the information in the user interface, when multiple aliases are available. The attribute "primary" is not intended to indicate the official name (e.g., the one indicated in official documents)

names.description: this list contains the names associated with the entity; there may be multiple variations of a name associated with the same entity (e.g., alias)

country.description: the country of the location (ISO3 format)

locations.description: this list contains all Locations associated with the entity, for example it may contain a reference or the place of birth

datasets.description: this field indicates the dataset from which the record originates

codes.description: this list contains all the codes associated with the entity; each object contains the actual code and the type of code associated (e.g., Passport, ID Document, LEI Code)

death_dates.description: this field contains the death dates associated with the entity, each object contains the day, month and year separated in three different fields as it's also possible to have a death_date object that only contains the year or only the year and the month (however the year will always be set). This object also contains an additional field inferred, this field is used when the death date is uncertain

gender.description: this field contains the gender of the entity (Male, Female, Unknown/not a person)

organization_details.description: this field contains the detail of the organization. Only if entity_type = 'L' the field can be valued; if valued, it will contain the details of the organization whose main properties have been expressed in the other fields

organization_subtype.description: defines the organization sub-type of the organization (e.g., central+direct, central+indirect, regional+direct, etc.)

organization_type.description: defines the organization type of the organization (e.g., State Owned Enterprise, Independent Authority, etc.)

sector.description: defines the sector of the organization using the Global Industry Classification Standard

population.description: represents the population of the organization (i.e., number of people resident in the municipality)

birth_dates.description: this field contains the birthdates associated with the entity; each object contains the day, month and year separated in three different fields as it's also possible to have a birth_date object that only contains the year or only the year and the month (however the year will always be set, birth dates never contain only the day or day and month with no year indicated). This object also contains an additional field "inferred", this field is used when the birthdate is uncertain (for example in cases where the source does only specify an age)

nationalities.description: this list contains the nationalities (as String) associated with the entity

photo.description: this field contains a list of photos associated with this entity

lang.description: language code of the recap, following the ISO 639-1 standard (e.g., en for English, it for Italian)

recap.description: the text of the summary in the specified language

recaps.description: this field contains a list of summaries (recaps) for the entity, each in a different language. Each entry includes a language code and the corresponding summary text

full_name.description: the full name of the entity

native.description: if set to true this field defines that this name is written in non-Latin characters

last_name.description: the last name of the entity

title.description: the title of the entity (Mr, Ms, etc.)

primary.description: if set to true this field defines that this name record is the primary of this entity. This is merely a suggestion for the visualization of the information in the user interface, when multiple aliases are available. The attribute "primary" is not intended to indicate the official name (e.g., the one indicated in official documents)

country.description: the country of the location (ISO3 format)

locations.description: this list contains all Locations associated with the entity, for example it may contain a reference or the place of birth

last_update.description: object that contains the last updated date (ISO date yyyy-mm-dd) of the entity and of all the entity's Dataset. It can be null when the last update date could not be recorded for technical reasons (e.g., when migrating to the new format)

get.description:
Initiates the process of credit score verification for a specific organization.
You can use the service by providing a tax code, a VAT number, or our ID obtained through the [Company](https://console.openapi.com/it/apis/company/documentation#docs-swaggerui) service
- Input Parameters: Organization details such as company ID tax code or VAT number.
- Output Parameters: Credit score details including organization name, risk score, and other relevant details.

parameters.name: vatCode_taxCode_or_id
parameters.description: Fiscal code, vat number or id company

requested_at.description: Date and time of the request.

risk_score.description: Risk score (graded from VERDE to ROSSO )

success.description: Indicates if the request was completed successfully.

example.message: Billing error message: Insufficient Credit in Wallet: 200 > 1.1935842939015E-12 code 810
id, fiscal_code or tax_code not valid

get.description:
Advances in the credit score verification process for a specific organization.
You can use the service by providing a tax code, a VAT number, or our ID obtained through the [Company](https://console.openapi.com/it/apis/company/documentation#docs-swaggerui) service
- Input Parameters: Organization details such as company ID tax code or VAT number.
- Output Parameters: Advanced credit score details including operational credit limits, rating evaluations, and risk score history.

parameters.name: vatCode_taxCode_or_id
parameters.description: Fiscal code, vat number or id company

operational_credit_limit.description: A credit limit estimate. Null is returned for both values in the absence of data from the Balance Sheet for the last 3 years.

rating.description: The scale ranges from A1 positive to C3 negative. Null is returned for both values in the absence of data from the Balance Sheet for the last 3 years.

requested_at.description: Date and time of the request.

risk_score.description: Risk score (graded from VERDE to ROSSO SCURO)

enum: analysis needs to be extended to related entities

risk_severity.description: An attribute to potentially use for sorting a list of subjects by risk score. Possible values range from 1 (low risk) to 990 (high risk).

success.description: Indicates if the request was completed successfully.

example.message: Billing error message: Insufficient Credit in Wallet: 200 > 1.1935842939015E-12 code 810
id, fiscal_code or tax_code not valid

get.description:
Provides detailed credit score information for a specific organization using a tax code, VAT number, or company ID. 
- **Input Parameters**: Organization details such as company ID, tax code, or VAT number.
- **Output Parameters**: 
  - Top credit score details including:
    - Operational credit limits
    - Rating evaluations
    - Risk score history
    - Public ratings
    - Financial positions and profiles

parameters.name: vatCode_taxCode_or_id
parameters.description: Fiscal code, VAT number or company ID

operational_credit_limit.description: A credit limit estimate. Null is returned for both values in the absence of data from the Balance Sheet for the last 3 years.

rating.description: The scale ranges from A1 positive to C3 negative. Null is returned for both values in the absence of data from the Balance Sheet for the last 3 years.

risk_score.description: Risk score (graded from VERDE to ROSSO SCURO)

industry_position.description: Quintile of belonging related to the sector. (e.g. Q3).

industry_position_description.description: Analysis of the company's evaluation indices (eg "Autocopertura delle immobilizzazioni")

industry_position_last_year_trend.description: Last year's trend of the industry position.

industry_position_total.description: Quintile of belonging related to the population (e.g. Q3)

industry_position_type.description: Type of indicators. The only result available is: "ind" ie index

requested_at.description: Date and time of the request.

success.description: Indicates if the request was completed successfully.

note.description: Durante questo periodo non sono emersi elementi rilevanti di modifica della valutazione: l'azienda mantiene una valutazione complessivamente invariata.

post.description:
This endpoint allows you to create a full kyc request on a subject (politically exposed person, adverse media, local politicians, legal enforcement, sanctions, whitelists). 

The response is 99 percent synchronous (state = 'COMPLETED') so no callback will be triggered. In the rare cases where the system behaves asynchronously (state = 'PENDING'), if set, a callback will be triggered when the request is processed and the state becomes 'COMPLETED' (otherwise, simply query the GET/WW-kyc-evidence endpoint to check your requests). 

Full is the only endpoint that allows you to download attachments such as documents and photos.

example.message: Billing error message: Insufficient Credit in Wallet: 200 > 1.1935842939015E-12 code 810

value.message: query not valid, must be an object
birthDate not valid, must be YYYY-MM-DD
entityType not valid, must be one of: I, L, W, VE, AC, NA
query required
name or firstName/lastName combination required
firstName required
lastName required

post.description:
This endpoint allows you to create a pep kyc request on a subject (politically exposed person, local politicians). 

The response is 99 percent synchronous (state = 'COMPLETED') so no callback will be triggered. In the rare cases where the system behaves asynchronously (state = 'PENDING'), if set, a callback will be triggered when the request is processed and the state becomes 'COMPLETED' (otherwise, simply query the GET/WW-kyc-evidence endpoint to check your requests).

example.message: Billing error message: Insufficient Credit in Wallet: 200 > 1.1935842939015E-12 code 810

value.message: query not valid, must be an object
birthDate not valid, must be YYYY-MM-DD
entityType not valid, must be one of: I, L, W, VE, AC, NA
query required
name or firstName/lastName combination required
firstName required
lastName required

post.description:
This endpoint allows you to create a sanction_list kyc request on a subject (legal enforcement, sanctions, whitelists). 

The response is 99 percent synchronous (state = 'COMPLETED') so no callback will be triggered. In the rare cases where the system behaves asynchronously (state = 'PENDING'), if set, a callback will be triggered when the request is processed and the state becomes 'COMPLETED' (otherwise, simply query the GET/WW-kyc-evidence endpoint to check your requests).

example.message: Billing error message: Insufficient Credit in Wallet: 200 > 1.1935842939015E-12 code 810

value.message: query not valid, must be an object
birthDate not valid, must be YYYY-MM-DD
entityType not valid, must be one of: I, L, W, VE, AC, NA
query required
name or firstName/lastName combination required
firstName required
lastName required

post.description:
This endpoint allows you to create a adverse_media kyc request on a subject (adverse media). 

The response is 99 percent synchronous (state = 'COMPLETED') so no callback will be triggered. In the rare cases where the system behaves asynchronously (state = 'PENDING'), if set, a callback will be triggered when the request is processed and the state becomes 'COMPLETED' (otherwise, simply query the GET/WW-kyc-evidence endpoint to check your requests).

example.message: Billing error message: Insufficient Credit in Wallet: 200 > 1.1935842939015E-12 code 810

value.message: query not valid, must be an object
birthDate not valid, must be YYYY-MM-DD
entityType not valid, must be one of: I, L, W, VE, AC, NA

get.description: This endpoint shows you the list of kyc requests that belong to your account

example.message: no requests related to your account

get.description: This endpoint returns the specific request identified by the id to check state and result

This endpoint allows you to do 3 things: 

- Request evidence for a specific entity using its id (entities generated from query performed with POST calls are found in the 'entities' field and each is identified by an id). You have a maximum of 10 days from when the request was created. 

- Download the document in PDF format with all the evidences for the chosen entity. Simply indicate the Content-Type header as application/pdf when requesting. This option is only valid for 'full' type requests. 

- Download entity photos in JPG format. Simply indicate the Content-Type header as image/jpeg when requesting and specify the photo id in the query string. This option is only valid for 'full' type requests.

value.message: no request matches this id: 60d1bfc731177b0a092cdfc2
entity EA8244402 not present in entities
photo test.jpg not present in EA8244402 entitiy
the request is still in PENDING state
the request did not produce any entities to choose from
operation not permitted, 10-day limit for requesting pdf exceeded
operation not permitted, PDF can only be downloaded on requests of type 'full'
operation not permitted, JPG can only be downloaded on requests of type 'full'
operation not permitted, 10-day limit for requesting jpg exceeded
operation not permitted, 10-day limit for requesting evidences exceeded

post.description:
This endpoint allows you to create a full kyc monitoring on a subject (politically exposed person, adverse media, local politicians, legal enforcement, sanctions, whitelists). 
Monitoring lasts for one year. Updates on a subject are received only when something changes. If a callback is set, we notify you of the change.

example.message: Billing error message: Insufficient Credit in Wallet: 200 > 1.1935842939015E-12 code 810

value.message: query not valid, must be an object
birthDate not valid, must be YYYY-MM-DD
entityType not valid, must be one of: I, L, W, VE, AC, NA
name required
query required
name or firstName/lastName combination required
firstName required
lastName required

delete.description: This endpoint allows you to delete a monitoring on a subject
